The director needs to schedule scenes from 5 movies with shooting days of 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days respectively. The release years are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015. The constraint is that no two consecutive scenes can come from movies released in the same decade. The decades are {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. The movie from 1994 belongs to {1990-1999}, the movies from 2004 and 2007 belong to {2000-2009}, and the movies from 2012 and 2015 belong to {2010-2019}. To minimize the total number of shooting days, we need to arrange the scenes in an order that alternates decades. One possible optimal sequence is 1994, 2012, 2004, 2015, 2007. The total number of shooting days is 4 + 10 + 6 + 12 + 8 = 30 days. This sequence satisfies the constraint and minimizes the total shooting days.  
******  
The director has a budget of ₹50 lakhs and the cost per day for each scene is ₹1 lakh, ₹1.5 lakhs, ₹2 lakhs, ₹2.5 lakhs, and ₹3 lakhs respectively. To maximize the number of completed scenes within the budget, we can formulate a linear programming problem. Let x1, x2, x3, x4, x5 represent the number of days each scene is shot. The objective is to maximize the number of scenes completed, which is the sum of binary variables indicating whether each scene is completed.
